records.description: Microsoft Copilot access through the University Microsoft licence, recommended when students are asked to engage with AI to reduce data and privacy concerns.
records.howToObtain: Log in with a University of Bristol email address.
records.costToUser: Free through the University Microsoft licence.

evidence.sourceTitle: What generative AI tools should I allow or encourage my students to use?
evidence.evidenceSnippet: We recommend for the time being using Copilot if you are asking your students to engage with AI in order to minimise data and privacy concerns.

records.universityName: The London School of Economics and Political Science (LSE)
records.description: Anthropic AI assistant available through LSE's Claude for Education partnership.
records.howToObtain: Request access through LSE guidance; once approved, use Claude via web and mobile app.

evidence.sourceTitle: Generative AI - LSE
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Claude is an AI assistant from Anthropic, available to you through LSE's Claude for Education partnership. It's useful for working through complex material, getting coding help, breaking down difficult concepts, and thinking through research questions.

records.universityName: The London School of Economics and Political Science (LSE)
records.description: Microsoft Copilot Chat for writing, summarising, researching, and thinking through ideas.
records.howToObtain: Sign in at copilot.microsoft.com with an LSE IT account.

evidence.sourceTitle: Generative AI - LSE
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Microsoft Copilot Chat is available to all LSE students through your institutional account. You can use it to help with writing, summarising, researching, and thinking through ideas.

records.description: CityUHK generative AI chatbot service providing staff and students access to GPT and DeepSeek models in a university environment.
records.howToObtain: Access through the CityUHK Mobile App or the IT and Library Services section on the CityUHK Portal.

evidence.sourceTitle: Generative AI | City University of Hong Kong
evidence.evidenceSnippet: We provide GPT-5 mini, GPT-5.2 (ChatGPT), DeepSeek R1, and DeepSeek V4 Flash to all staff members and students free to use with monthly usage quota enforced.

records.description: Microsoft Copilot for Enterprise included in CityUHK's Microsoft 365 licence.
records.howToObtain: Access through CityUHK Microsoft 365 services.
records.costToUser: No extra cost; included in the Microsoft 365 licence.

evidence.sourceTitle: Microsoft Copilot for Enterprise: Now Available for All Staff and Students
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Microsoft Copilot for Enterprise (Copilot) is included in the Microsoft 365 (M365) license at no extra cost and has no monthly usage caps.

records.description: Paid Microsoft 365 Copilot add-on integrating with Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, and other M365 apps.
records.howToObtain: Requires an additional licence approved by the Head of Department; staff should contact their department DNA.

evidence.sourceTitle: Microsoft Copilot for Enterprise: Now Available for All Staff and Students
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Copilot also offers a standalone paid version – Microsoft Copilot for M365 (M365 Copilot), that integrates seamlessly with Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook.

records.description: Copilot side window for browser-based assistance with protected access through the University account.

records.costToUser: Free for University students and staff.

evidence.sourceTitle: Generative Artificial Intelligence (Gen AI) - University of Auckland
evidence.evidenceSnippet: These tools have been approved by our Cybersecurity team and give you protected access to: OpenAI’s GPT-4 Large Language Model.

records.description: Google Gemini for drafting, summarising, brainstorming, and natural-language questions.

evidence.sourceTitle: Generative Artificial Intelligence (Gen AI) - University of Auckland
evidence.evidenceSnippet: We recommend you use Google Gemini to help you draft, summarise, brainstorm, and answer questions in natural language.

records.description: AI-powered research assistant for exploring and asking questions about documents and sources.

evidence.sourceTitle: Generative Artificial Intelligence (Gen AI) - University of Auckland
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Use Notebook LM as an AI-powered research assistant that helps you explore and ask questions about your own documents and sources.
